description:
You are a deployment engineer who has to roll-out a routine Python dependency
    upgrade for an internal project.

    Current project layout
      /home/user/app/
          requirements.txt      (contains old versions)
          <other-project-files>

    Goal
      1. Create a *virtual environment* called “deploy_venv” **inside the project
         directory**:  /home/user/app/deploy_venv
      2. Activate that virtual environment and upgrade *pip* itself to the newest
         version available from PyPI.
      3. Inside that activated venv, install the **exact** package versions
         specified below:
            • flask==2.3.2
            • requests==2.31.0
      4. Produce a human-readable log file that documents the upgrade.

    Required log file
      • Path:  /home/user/app/deploy_logs/upgrade_report.log
      • Directory /home/user/app/deploy_logs/ must be created if it does not yet
        exist.
      • Permissions: regular user-writable (default umask is fine).

      VERY STRICT FORMAT (the automated grader checks this
      character-for-character, including case and spacing):

        Line 1  → an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, exactly this `date` format:  
                  YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ  
                  Example: 2025-01-31T15:27:42Z  
                  (Your actual timestamp will naturally differ.)

        Line 2  → flask==2.3.2

        Line 3  → requests==2.31.0

      No extra lines, spaces, or trailing whitespace are allowed.

    Verification Hints
      • After the task, the grader will:
          – Check that /home/user/app/deploy_venv/ exists and contains the venv’s
            standard bin/, lib/, etc. sub-directories.
          – `python -m pip show flask` and `python -m pip show requests` inside
            that venv must report the exact versions above.
          – Read /home/user/app/deploy_logs/upgrade_report.log and verify:
              1. Line-count equals 3.
              2. Line-1 matches the ISO-8601 UTC pattern.
              3. Line-2 is literally “flask==2.3.2”.
              4. Line-3 is literally “requests==2.31.0”.

    Starting point
      • /home/user/app/requirements.txt is already present and currently contains:
            flask==2.2.0
            requests==2.28.0
        You do NOT need to modify this file; it is just historical context.
      • No virtual environment exists yet.
      • The default python3 interpreter is available system-wide.

    Your task as the AI assistant
      Guide the shell in real time to:
        • create the venv,
        • activate it,
        • upgrade pip,
        • install the specified package versions,
        • create the deploy_logs directory,
        • generate the correctly-formatted upgrade_report.log.

    The entire job can be done with three to four well-placed shell commands.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth state that the grader will enforce -->
    <required_paths>
        /home/user/app/deploy_venv/
        /home/user/app/deploy_logs/upgrade_report.log
    </required_paths>

    <log_file>/home/user/app/deploy_logs/upgrade_report.log</log_file>

    <!-- The grader will allow any valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp for line-1.
         Lines 2-3 MUST match exactly the following. -->
    <log_line_2>flask==2.3.2</log_line_2>
    <log_line_3>requests==2.31.0</log_line_3>

    <required_packages>
        flask==2.3.2
        requests==2.31.0
    </required_packages>
name: task_000000_960fedcd
